text.content: In agricultural operations, your tires represent a significant investment—and their lifespan directly impacts your bottom line. Unlike regulated highway tires, agricultural tires operate under unique conditions where soil compaction, farming operations intensity, and maintenance practices collectively determine tire longevity. At MAXAM, we believe understanding these factors helps you maximize service life while maintaining peak performance and durability across all your equipment.

rich_text.plain_text: In agricultural operations, your tires represent a significant investment—and their lifespan directly impacts your bottom line. Unlike regulated highway tires, agricultural tires operate under unique conditions where soil compaction, farming operations intensity, and maintenance practices collectively determine tire longevity. Ag Industry Lifespan Benchmarks

text.content: While individual results vary based on operating conditions, industry data reveals general replacement patterns:

rich_text.plain_text: While individual results vary based on operating conditions, industry data reveals general replacement patterns:
High-Horsepower Tractors

text.content: MFWD & 4WD tractor tires (100+ HP): Typically replaced at 5-year intervals

rich_text.plain_text: MFWD & 4WD tractor tires (100+ HP): Typically replaced at 5-year intervals
Representing 65% of market replacement cycles

text.content: Regular road use accelerates tread wear

rich_text.plain_text: Regular road use accelerates tread wear
Harvesting Equipment

text.content: Combine/ Harvester tires: Average 10-year service life

rich_text.plain_text: Combine/ Harvester tires: Average 10-year service life
Comprising 10% of replacement market

text.content: Subject to seasonal intensive heavy loads

rich_text.plain_text: Subject to seasonal intensive heavy loads
Application Equipment

text.content: Sprayer and implement tires: 1-2 year replacement cycles

rich_text.plain_text: Sprayer and implement tires: 1-2 year replacement cycles
Accounting for 15% of market demand

Environmental Factors

text.content: Stubble damage to tire tread, lugs, and sidewall

rich_text.plain_text: Stubble damage to tire tread, lugs, and sidewall

text.content: Direct sunlight exposure causing ozone degradation

rich_text.plain_text: Direct sunlight exposure causing ozone degradation

text.content: Soil chemistry and moisture affecting rubber compounds

rich_text.plain_text: Soil chemistry and moisture affecting rubber compounds

text.content: Adjusted tire inflation not working or tire tread depth (increased wear) reducing operational traction, great fuel consumption, or increased machine slip.

cells.plain_text: Adjusted tire inflation not working or tire tread depth (increased wear) reducing operational traction, great fuel consumption, or increased machine slip.

text.content: Tires may be too worn for the soil conditions or crop conditions of the operations. If tires are exceeding a 15% slip rate;

cells.plain_text: Tires may be too worn for the soil conditions or crop conditions of the operations. If tires are exceeding a 15% slip rate;

text.content: Loss of traction due to poor air valve maintenance or tire cuts resulting in constant air loss.

cells.plain_text: Loss of traction due to poor air valve maintenance or tire cuts resulting in constant air loss.

text.content: Tire damages from operational use that result in tire leakage or loss of air required, either repair your tire or

cells.plain_text: Tire damages from operational use that result in tire leakage or loss of air required, either repair your tire or

text.content: Cuts or Damages (stubble) in the Tread or Sidewall

cells.plain_text: Cuts or Damages (stubble) in the Tread or Sidewall

text.content: Tire cuts, exposed casing cables, large damage, or deformities on the tread/sidewall are indicative of either external or internal damages impacting safe usage.

cells.plain_text: Tire cuts, exposed casing cables, large damage, or deformities on the tread/sidewall are indicative of either external or internal damages impacting safe usage.

text.content: Evaluate the tires for damages that could impair operations or result in equipment damage while in operation = imminent failure or loss of tire integrity;

cells.plain_text: Evaluate the tires for damages that could impair operations or result in equipment damage while in operation = imminent failure or loss of tire integrity;

text.content: Tires are exhibiting uneven tire wear or more than 10% difference in tread depth on the same axle can result in a loss of traction, increased slip, increased fuel consumption, or can impact gear ratios.

cells.plain_text: Tires are exhibiting uneven tire wear or more than 10% difference in tread depth on the same axle can result in a loss of traction, increased slip, increased fuel consumption, or can impact gear ratios.

text.content: Tires have uneven wear that can precipitate a loss of traction or increased fuel consumption and /or can also impact the gear ratio of a front wheel assist tractor;

cells.plain_text: Tires have uneven wear that can precipitate a loss of traction or increased fuel consumption and /or can also impact the gear ratio of a front wheel assist tractor;

text.content: Tires that have more than 5% difference in overall diameter mounted side by side or on the same axle can lead to a loss of traction, increases slip, can generate gear ratio issues, or increase soil compaction due to load shift.

cells.plain_text: Tires that have more than 5% difference in overall diameter mounted side by side or on the same axle can lead to a loss of traction, increases slip, can generate gear ratio issues, or increase soil compaction due to load shift.

text.content: Tire diameters need to be as closely matched as possible to prevent excessive slip / limit any gear ratio mechanical issues. Overall diameter differences can shift the axle load to the larger tire, increasing soil compaction;

cells.plain_text: Tire diameters need to be as closely matched as possible to prevent excessive slip / limit any gear ratio mechanical issues. Overall diameter differences can shift the axle load to the larger tire, increasing soil compaction;

text.content: Tires stored or equipment parked in the open can achieve excess sunlight or UV rays that can lead to ozone cracking that will degrade tire life or tread integrity.

cells.plain_text: Tires stored or equipment parked in the open can achieve excess sunlight or UV rays that can lead to ozone cracking that will degrade tire life or tread integrity.

text.content: Inspect tires in open storage or parked equipment for ozone cracks that expose internal materials such as working belts or casing materials;

cells.plain_text: Inspect tires in open storage or parked equipment for ozone cracks that expose internal materials such as working belts or casing materials;
